title: Use race-aware purged validation instead of row-mixed CV
summary: This is panel time-series data: adjacent laps from the same driver/race are highly dependent.
rationale: This is panel time-series data: adjacent laps from the same driver/race are highly dependent. Public guidance for time-ordered and grouped CV indicates that random or weakly grouped folds can overstate AUC by leaking same-race structure.
implementation_hint: Benchmark `LeaveOneGroupOut` on `group=Year|Race` and a blocked/rolling scheme by year-race order. Keep current models, but only trust feature gains that survive strict race holdout. Compare CV-LB correlation before changing features.
expected_effect: More honest local CV and fewer leaderboard regressions; may expose which current engineered features are exploiting within-race leakage.
risk: CV score will likely drop, and if the test set contains many familiar race contexts, the stricter split can be slightly pessimistic.
